items.title: UK PM Keir Starmer resigns, triggering a leadership race
items.body:
British Prime Minister **Keir Starmer announced his resignation** on Monday.

- A contest to choose his successor is now under way
- Markets and allies are watching the direction of the next government

> Expect a stretch of political uncertainty in Westminster.

items.title: Alan Greenspan, longtime Fed chair, dies at 100
items.body:
**Alan Greenspan**, who led the U.S. Federal Reserve for nearly two decades, has died at age 100.

- Chaired the Fed from 1987 to 2006
- Shaped an era of monetary policy and market debate

> Tributes poured in from across economics and politics.

items.title: US and Iran agree on a 60-day roadmap to a final deal
items.body:
Washington and Tehran say they have agreed on a **roadmap** to reach a final agreement within 60 days.

- Follows the earlier framework to end the war
- Negotiators still face thorny verification questions

> Oil markets stay watchful as details emerge.

items.title: World Cup 2026 rolls on with upsets and breakout debutants
items.body:
The World Cup keeps delivering drama.

- First-time qualifiers are punching above their weight
- Several favorites have stumbled in the group stage

> Knockout-round spots are tightening by the day.
